meta.source: Aluminium Market Intelligence Engine — public sources

soWhat.headline: Aluminium's latest LME official settled firmly higher - cash +$27.50 to $3,183.50 on 21-Jul with the cash-3M backwardation widening to +$13 and stock at 278,275 t, the lowest since 2022 - as Strait of Hormuz shipping stays largely stalled after a fresh run of tanker attacks, even while a 10-day ceasefire proposal keeps the near-term skew two-sided.
bullets: What moved: the latest published LME official (21-Jul) settled higher across the board - cash +$27.50 to $3,183.50, 3M +$23.00 to $3,170.50 - clearing the $3,160 pivot, while the cash-3M backwardation steepened to +$13.00 and LME stock fell another 1,500 t to 278,275 t.

Why it matters: Strait of Hormuz traffic is largely stalled after repeated tanker attacks (the crude tanker Al Bahyah was hit Tuesday, one seafarer killed), keeping roughly 9% of global primary supply at risk. The widening backwardation and record-low stocks show the physical market tightening, not just headline risk.

The two-sided catalyst: mediators have put a 10-day ceasefire proposal in front of Washington and Tehran. A durable truce would release the war-risk premium and reopen Gulf shipping; a collapse re-escalates. It is the swing variable for the next several sessions.

The offset and the watch: EGA's Al Taweelah alumina restart (full production since 10-Jul) and record Chinese exports (June 711 kt) cap the upside. Watch whether LME stock keeps drawing below 278,275 t, whether the backwardation keeps steepening on the next official, and the $3,230/$3,260 resistance stack.

kpis.label: LME Cash settlement
kpis.note: 21-Jul OFFICIAL (Westmetall) $3,183.50 vs 20-Jul $3,156.00 = +$27.50 (+0.87%). Latest PUBLISHED official at this Wed 22-Jul evening refresh; Westmetall posts the 22-Jul official with a session lag (LME officials print 13:20 London). avg = June-2026 monthly mean ($3,458.6).

kpis.label: LME 3-Month
kpis.note: 21-Jul official $3,170.50 vs 20-Jul $3,147.50 (+$23.00, +0.73%) - latest published official. Cleared the $3,160 pivot; resistance stack $3,230 (MA20) then $3,260 (.382 of the Jun-Jul decline). avg = June-2026 3M mean ($3,438.9).

kpis.label: Cash-3M spread
kpis.note: BACKWARDATION widened to +$13.00 (cash $3,183.50 over 3M $3,170.50) on the 21-Jul official, up from +$8.50 on 20-Jul. Positive cash-3M = backwardation = prompt tightness, consistent with LME stock at 278,275 t, the lowest since 2022. The steepening front confirms the physical squeeze building alongside the Gulf shipping disruption.

kpis.label: LME stock (t)
kpis.note: 278,275 t at the 21-Jul official, down 1,500 t on the day and roughly -45.4% year-to-date from 509,250 t on 02-Jan-2026. Below 280,000 t and the lowest since 2022. Draws have been continuous through July; SHFE stockpiles are also declining.

benchmark.commentary: The 21-Jul official is the latest PUBLISHED LME official (cash $3,183.50 / 3M $3,170.50 / stock 278,275 t) at this Wed 22-Jul evening refresh; Westmetall posts the 22-Jul official with a session lag. This was a real settlement move higher - cash +$27.50 (+0.87%) and 3M +$23.00 (+0.73%) - that cleared the $3,160 pivot and held the rebound off the early-July four-month low near $3,085. Copper firmed in parallel toward $13,800/t on the LME. The geopolitical tape stayed hot: Strait of Hormuz traffic…

drivers.title: Hormuz: enforcement, not just threat - the dominant driver
drivers.detail: Iran disabled two oil tankers attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz without permission on Monday 20-Jul, the first direct interdiction of individual merchant vessels in this conflict. A third US service member was killed since Friday, US strikes reached a ninth consecutive day, and Iran struck Kuwaiti power/desalination sites and Bahrain. With roughly 9% of global primary aluminium supply originating in the Gulf, war-risk cover and routing costs are building rather than fading.
drivers.source: NPR - U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed (20-Jul-2026)

drivers.title: US Section 232 at 50% - the administered floor under the US duty-paid premium
drivers.detail: Products made entirely or almost entirely of aluminium pay 50% on their full customs value under the proclamation signed 01-Jun-2026, effective 08-Jun-2026 through 31-Dec-2027, with CBP guidance issued 05-Jun-2026 (CSMS #68855869). At an LME around $3,150/t this is roughly $1,575/t of pure policy cost embedded in US duty-paid metal, mechanically decoupling the US premium from the rest of the world.
drivers.source: U.S. Customs and Border Protection - Section 232 Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum FAQs

drivers.title: Europe: war premium fading against a structural floor
drivers.detail: Platts assessed duty-unpaid P1020A in-warehouse Rotterdam at $525-560/t (mid $542.50) on 03-Jul, down from $600-625/t on 13-May as the Middle East war premium receded. The floor beneath it is structural rather than sentiment-driven: the 6% EU import duty, CBAM, and idled European capacity. Liquidity remains thin as Rotterdam competes with Asia for units.
drivers.source: S&P Global - TRADE REVIEW: European aluminum market seeks floor as Middle East war premium fades (07-Jul-2026)

drivers.title: Japan: Q3 settled at a decade high, Q4 negotiations open against it
drivers.detail: The Q3-2026 MJP CIF Japan premium settled at $395/t (assessed 06-Jul), a decade high and roughly 12% above Q2's $350-353/t. Q4 talks open around September against that anchor; the swing variable is whether Hormuz disruption persists at current intensity or a diplomatic track restores flow.
drivers.source: S&P Global Platts Q3-2026 MJP settlement $395/t, via IndexBox

items.name: Alumina FOB Australia (SMM/Fastmarkets)
items.note: $328/t FOB Western Australia (SMM, 09-Jul) — down from ~$338 as the EGA Al Taweelah refinery restart (10-Jul) ramps and the ex-China surplus deepens. The SMM Alumina Index eased to ¥2,730.17/t (−¥12.8 w/w). Gulf-routed cargoes still carry elevated war-risk cover given the further-escalated Kuwaiti energy-infrastructure strikes. Verify SMM/Fastmarkets.

items.name: Alumina API / Platts region
items.note: Ex-China benchmark region ~$303/t — easing on the building surplus (Q1 avg $306.9; latest public reference, no fresher print surfaced). SMM flags ex-China metallurgical-grade output down ~6% y/y in June — a partial offset to the EGA restart. Verify Platts.

items.name: SHFE-region alumina (SMM Alumina Index)
items.note: SMM Alumina Index ¥2,730.17/t, down ¥12.8/t week-on-week; regional spot Shandong ¥2,710–2,780, Henan ¥2,730–2,800, Shanxi ¥2,750–2,800, Guangxi ¥2,600–2,680, Guizhou ¥2,760–2,800. Futures trade below moving averages with port stocks building even as the Gulf strait squeeze still weighs on regional refinery feed (~60% of Gulf alumina imports transit Hormuz).

items.name: Platts FOB Australia (futures)
items.note: Alumina FOB Australia (Platts) futures ~$309.4/t (-0.5% on the day, CME/TradingView, 21-Jul) - easing on the building ex-China surplus as the EGA restart ramps.

items.name: Bauxite (Guinea, CIF China)
items.note: ~$68 CIF China (FOB Guinea ~$38, 45/3 grade); oversupply persists — Metro Mining shipped a record 1.8 Mt in Q2 (+7% y/y; 2026 guidance 6.6–7.1 Mt held). Guinea is reportedly considering capping 2026 exports near 150 Mt (vs 178 Mt shipped in 2025, a ~15.7% cut) targeting >$100/dmt, but no formal decree had been issued as of end-June; no fresher public print surfaced over the weekend.

items.name: Caustic soda (flakes ≥98%, FOB NE Asia)
items.note: ~$500–510 FOB NE Asia; CFR Asia ~$380–560 — major refining reagent (ChemAnalyst/ChemTradeAsia); no fresher public print over the weekend.

items.name: Lime / quicklime (CaO)
items.note: No liquid global index — largely regional/contract; indicative range (causticisation reagent); no fresher public print over the weekend.

hormuz.detail: Overnight the US flew a 9th consecutive night of strikes on Iran and is reinforcing with F-16s (Germany) and F-35s (UK). Wire reporting puts Strait transits at as few as ~3 vessels per 24 hours, versus a normal ~110/day pre-war; the IRGC claims to have intercepted 4 further vessels. ~80–85% of Gulf aluminium exports and ~60% of regional alumina imports use this waterway.

events.title: FRESH TANKER ATTACK
events.detail: FRESH TANKER ATTACK: Iran attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday, the latest in a run of attacks that has largely stalled strait traffic. Roughly 80-85% of Gulf aluminium exports and ~60% of regional alumina imports use this waterway (CBSNews; CNBC).

events.title: NEW FRONT - HOUTHI EMBARGO
events.detail: NEW FRONT - HOUTHI EMBARGO: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis declared a maritime embargo / naval blockade against Saudi Arabia, threatening to open a new front and widen the shipping-risk zone beyond Hormuz (CNBC).

events.title: STRIKES TO 11TH NIGHT
events.detail: STRIKES TO 11TH NIGHT: The US completed an 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran; the US has moved to blockade Iranian ports as Washington seeks control of the Strait of Hormuz (Washington Post; CBSNews).

events.title: CEASEFIRE PROPOSAL
events.detail: CEASEFIRE PROPOSAL: Regional mediators presented Washington and Tehran with a 10-day ceasefire proposal - a potential de-escalation path, though large divisions remain and the June MoU already collapsed once (CNBC).

events.title: LME OFFICIAL FIRMER
events.detail: LME OFFICIAL FIRMER: The 20-Jul official ticked up to cash $3,156.00 / 3M $3,147.50 (stock 279,775 t); the cash-3M backwardation widened to +$8.50. The 21-Jul session traded to ~$3,167/t (+0.93%) (Westmetall; Trading Economics).

events.title: SUPPLY RELIEF
events.detail: SUPPLY RELIEF: EGA resumed full alumina production at Al Taweelah on 10-Jul after a three-and-a-half-month outage, targeting 50% of plant capacity within days and full capability by end-2026. The refinery produced 2.4 Mt of alumina in 2025 (EGA).

news.title: The US-Iran conflict entered an 11th night of strikes and Iran hit another tanker in the Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday, while Yemen's Houthis declared a maritime embargo against Saudi Arabia - a potential new front. The US is moving to blockade Iranian ports as Washington seeks control of the strait. Hormuz traffic is largely stalled.

Regional mediators presented Washington and Tehran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire. Large divisions remain and the June-14 memorandum of understanding already collapsed once in July, but a durable truce would release the war-risk premium and reopen Gulf shipping - the key two-sided catalyst for the flat price.

EGA's Al Taweelah refinery has been at full alumina production since 10-Jul, targeting 50% of capacity within days and full capability by end-2026. At 2.4 Mt in 2025 it supplies close to half of EGA's alumina need - the only material supply-side relief currently visible and a key reason the flat price is ranging rather than spiking.

LME stock fell to 279,775 t on the 20-Jul official, the lowest since 2022 and roughly 45% below the 509,250 t start-of-year level, with SHFE stockpiles also declining. Cash-3M widened to a +$8.50 backwardation - prompt tightness, not surplus.

Norsk Hydro released Q2 2026 results this morning (22-Jul, 07:00 CEST) - the first European producer P&L covering a full quarter of the disruption regime and the cleanest public read on European smelter power costs and regional premium pass-through. Guidance into the quarter had pointed to adjusted EBITDA around NOK 8.7-9.0bn.

peers.headline: Revenue $3.97bn (+24% q/q, highest in nearly a decade); adj. EPS $2.12 (MISS vs ~$2.31 consensus); adj. net income $562m; adj. EBITDA $901m (from $595m in Q1); Aluminum segment RECORD EBITDA $1.073bn at 32.3% margin.
peers.readthrough: First producer P&L under the Hormuz-disruption regime: record aluminium margins and near-decade-high revenue on higher LME + strong US Midwest premiums, but adj. EPS missed on higher costs — notably ~$35m more Section-232 tariff on Canadian metal into the US — and the shares fell. South32 upstream acquisition ($4.1bn u…

peers.headline: Q2 2026 results released this morning (22-Jul, 07:00 CEST). Guidance into the quarter pointed to adjusted EBITDA ~NOK 8.7-9.0bn, with ~67% of Q2 primary output hedged near $3,000/t and realised premiums guided at ~$530-580/t. Final reported figures were still being digested at this ~06:05 London refresh.
peers.readthrough: First European producer P&L covering a full quarter of the Hormuz-disruption regime - the read on Norwegian/European smelter power costs (Brent near $90, TTF gas ~EUR 58/MWh) and regional premium realisation. Hedging near $3,000/t caps the upside capture vs a spot LME above $3,150.

peers.headline: Adj. EPS $1.63 (beat $1.56); revenue $649m; net income $327m (incl. $288m Hawesville sale gain).
peers.readthrough: Higher LME + strong US Midwest premiums; Q2 adj. EBITDA guided $315–335m.

peers.headline: Net profit $200.3m (+316% YoY); EPS 53 fils; sales −17% on Gulf supply-chain disruption.
peers.readthrough: LME averaged $3,195/t (+22% YoY) drove profit surge; volumes hit by regional conflict; ~19% capacity in controlled shutdown — restart path improves post-truce.

peers.headline: Net profit SAR 1.63bn (~$436m, +5.5% YoY); revenue SAR 8.79bn (+3.2%); EBITDA $964m.
peers.readthrough: Strong pricing across commodities; partly offset by lower phosphate volumes; 2026 capex set >$4bn.

peers.headline: H1 copper-equivalent output +3% y/y; aluminium division improved; strong bauxite recovery; FY aluminium guidance UNCHANGED at 3.25–3.45 Mt; Pilbara Q2 shipments 89 Mt (+5%).
peers.readthrough: First upstream print of the Q2 season (this morning): Western volumes are intact — the Gulf disruption is expressing itself in prices, freight and premia rather than output. Kitimat/AP60 ramp continues; H1 financial results due 13-Aug.

peers.headline: Hillside Al ~538kt (9M, FY26 guide 720kt); Brazil Alumina record 1,060kt (9M); Worsley steady.
peers.readthrough: Alumina sales −15% q/q on availability/timing; annual guidance intact despite cyclone gas disruption.

peers.headline: Hindalco PAT ₹5,283cr (+66% YoY); India Al upstream EBITDA margin ~46%; Novelis FY26 adj. EBITDA $1.6bn (−9%).
peers.readthrough: Record India operations; Novelis pressured by Oswego outage (~$104m) and tariffs (~$143m).

peers.headline: Net profit guided RMB 5.30–5.59bn (+50–58% YoY).
peers.readthrough: Strong on higher Al prices; China output near self-imposed 45 Mt capacity cap.

peers.headline: Underlying profit $6.2bn (+>20%); underlying EBITDA $15.5bn (+25%, 58% margin); interim div 73c.
peers.readthrough: Copper-led strength; broad base-metals read-across positive for the complex.

peers.headline: FY25 results; copper & aluminium average prices +9% YoY; zinc metal +4%.
peers.readthrough: Resilient structural demand for Cu/Al cited; broadly constructive sector tone.

peers.headline: FY25 net loss $455m (vs $803m profit in 2024) on higher costs & lower output.
peers.readthrough: Analysts expect stronger H1 2026 on higher prices; Asia pivot amid fractured trade flows.

peers.headline: Guides H1-26 net profit ¥1.8–1.95bn, +154.4–175.6% YoY.
peers.readthrough: Credits industry-chain cost reduction, aluminium prices fluctuating at highs, high-value-added deep-processing mix and energy-saving retrofits — the first sizeable China H1 pre-announcement of the season and a positive read-across for Chinese producer margins.

outlook.consensus: TIGHT MARKET, TWO-SIDED CATALYST. The flat price is firming with conviction: the 21-Jul official rose $27.50 to cash $3,183.50 and 3M $23.00 to $3,170.50, clearing the $3,160 pivot, while the curve backwardation widened to +$13.00 - the curve confirming the physical tightness. Escalation continues - stalled Hormuz traffic, a fresh tanker attack and a Houthi embargo threat keep roughly 9% of global primary supply at risk - but two forces cap the upside: EGA's Al Taweelah alumina restart (full pro…
catalysts: Today, from 07:00 CEST: Norsk Hydro Q2 2026 results (released this morning; webcast 08:30 CEST) - first European producer P&L covering a full quarter of the disruption regime; read on European smelter power costs and regional premium pass-through

Today, 13:20 London: Next LME official ring - the first settled print able to reflect the 11th-night strikes and Tuesday's tanker attack; watch whether the cash-3M backwardation steepens beyond +$8.50
Daily: LME warehouse stock report - whether draws continue below 280,100 t, the lowest level since 2022
Through Q3: EGA Al Taweelah ramp from 50% of capacity toward full capability by end-2026 - the principal supply-side offset in the current balance
Around September: Q4-2026 MJP CIF Japan premium negotiations open against the decade-high Q3 settlement of $395/t

Days: The 10-day ceasefire proposal in front of Washington and Tehran - traction would reopen Gulf shipping and release the war premium; collapse would re-escalate. The single biggest two-sided swing factor

risks.title: Hormuz / Gulf escalation: an 11th night of US strikes, a fresh tanker attack on 21-Jul, and a Houthi maritime-embargo threat against Saudi Arabia keep roughly 9% of global primary aluminium supply at risk with strait traffic largely stalled; a widening into ge…
Inventory floor: LME stock at 279,775 t is the lowest since 2022 and roughly -45% YTD with SHFE also drawing - there is no inventory buffer to absorb a fresh supply interruption
Energy cost squeeze: Brent above $90/bbl with EU gas following on a lag compresses marginal-producer margins in Europe and Asia and biases capacity toward curtailment rather than restart
Al Taweelah ramp disappoints or is interrupted - the alumina relief is the principal offset holding the flat price down and it is a ramp toward end-2026, not delivered tonnage
Demand destruction: sustained elevated prices plus the 50% Section 232 rate on US imports encourage substitution and scrap displacement, particularly in packaging and construction

Two-sided diplomacy: a durable 10-day ceasefire would reopen Gulf shipping and collapse the war-risk premium sharply (downside to price); a collapse like the June MoU would re-escalate. A Section 232 rate change would likewise reprice the US premium complex qu…

caveats: Report refreshed Wed 22-Jul-2026 (evening London). The benchmark table and lme_series carry the latest PUBLISHED LME official, the 21-Jul settlement (cash $3,183.50 / 3M $3,170.50 / stock 278,275 t) from Westmetall; the 22-Jul official posts on Westmetall with a session lag. Official settlements are the reference for the benchmark table. · MIXED SOURCE BASIS ON THE BOARD: the aluminium level on the metals board is set to the LME 21-Jul 3-month official (Westmetall) for a clean, consistent basis; copper is the latest LME 3M level (~$13,800/t); nickel, zinc and lead are the latest publicly reported prints (Trading Economics, dated 21-Jul). Official and traded prices are different reference points and will not tie out exactly. · GEOPOLITICAL DEVELOPMENTS ARE FAST-MOVING and largely post-date any settled LME official. The tanker attacks, the Houthi embargo threat and the 10-day ceasefire proposal continue to evolve; treat the flat price as lagging the news flow, and note the ceasefire proposal makes the near-term skew two-sided. · PREMIUM ASSESSMENTS ARE PERIODIC, NOT DAILY: MJP settles quarterly, Rotterdam duty-unpaid and US Midwest are assessed by Platts/Fastmarkets on their own schedules. The figures shown are the latest PUBLIC prints (MJP Q3 settled $395/t on 06-Jul). They are not daily marks. · ALL CONTENT IS PUBLIC MARKET INFORMATION for situational awareness only. No confidential, internal, company-specific or position data is included, and nothing here is trading, hedging or investment advice. Elliott Wave analysis is technical context, not a forecast or recommendation.
